{"covered_dims": {"R1.6": 0.9, "R1.2": 0.3}}

Tightened the prose: removed filler clauses ("that we have used," "which we considered to be"), broke run-on sentences, and replaced hedged self-reference with direct statements. Content and claims unchanged.

---

**Revised text:**

We obtained the dataset from a climate-observation repository, accessed with permission from the data providers. It contains historical temperature and precipitation measurements at fine spatial resolution over several decades.

We applied standard preprocessing to the raw measurements to prepare them for regression analysis. The resulting cleaned dataset served as input to the modeling stage.

Our regression model uses temperature and precipitation as predictors of regional crop yield, aggregated annually. We fit the model by ordinary least squares; standard residual diagnostics indicated that this specification was appropriate.

The estimated coefficients suggest that temperature has a larger effect on yield than precipitation in the study region. The magnitude varies across sub-regions, however, and the ranking of the two predictors may be sensitive to the sub-region considered.

The results are broadly consistent with prior studies, though a more systematic comparison would strengthen this part of the analysis.

---

Note: I preserved your hedges where they convey real uncertainty (sub-regional variation, comparison with prior work) but removed redundant hedging on the methods. If you want a more assertive register, "broadly consistent" and "appropriate" can be sharpened further.
